content.problemStatement:
You will be given two arrays AAA and BBB, each of length NNN. For some value KKK, let's call an array CCC `good` if it satisfies the following conditions:


0‚Č§Ci‚Č§Bi0\le C_i \le B_i0‚Č§Ci‚Äč‚Č§Bi‚Äč for (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N)
**At least** KKK elements in the array must be equal to 000.
‚ąĎi=1NAi=‚ąĎi=1NCi\sum_{i=1}^N A_i = \sum_{i=1}^N C_i ‚ąĎi=1N‚ÄčAi‚Äč=‚ąĎi=1N‚ÄčCi‚Äč
Among all the `good` arrays CCC, let result(K)result(K)result(K) denote the **minimum** value of ‚ąĎi=1N‚ą£Ai‚ąíCi‚ą£\sum_{i=1}^N |A_i-C_i|‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ąíCi‚Äč‚ą£. If no good array is possible, result(K)=‚ąí1result(K) = -1result(K)=‚ąí1.

Find the value result(K)result(K)result(K) for all (0‚Č§K<N)(0\le K \lt N)(0‚Č§K<N).


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, the array AAA.
The third line contains NNN integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, the array BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers, result(K)result(K)result(K) for (0‚Č§K<N)(0 \le K \lt N)(0‚Č§K<N).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 100100100.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

[10,1,4,1,5][10, 1, 4, 1, 5][10,1,4,1,5] is a good array with at least 000 zeroes and gives the minimum value for result(0)result(0)result(0).
[10,0,5,1,5][10, 0, 5, 1, 5][10,0,5,1,5] is a good array with at least 111 zeroes and gives the minimum value for result(1)result(1)result(1).
No good array is possible for the given constraints when K=2,3,K = 2, 3,K=2,3, or 444.

Test case 222:

[7,7,9][7, 7, 9][7,7,9] is a good array with at least 000 zeroes and gives the minimum value of result(0)result(0)result(0).
No good array is possible when K=1K = 1K=1 or 222.
